mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Range Rover 4.4 V8 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Range Rover 4.4 V8 engine costs £4,000–£6,500 supply only (AJ-V8 era), or £3,000–£5,500 (M62 era). Fitting adds £1,400–£2,200. These are specialist-only replacements — the engine must match the vehicle's year. A timing chain guide job on the M62 costs £2,000–£4,000.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Range Rover 4.4 V8 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: That depends on which engine your L322 has. The BMW M62 (2002–2005) is a fine V8 but has a well-documented timing chain guide failure risk — at 60,000–100,000 miles, the plastic guides shatter. The Jaguar AJ-V8 (2005–2009) is significantly more reliable, but coolant crossover pipes are a known weakness. Post-2005 is the better bet.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Range Rover 4.4 V8?

acceptedAnswer.text: BMW M62: timing chain guide failure (cold-start rattle, £2,000–£4,000 repair). Jaguar AJ-V8: coolant crossover pipe failure (coolant loss, £800–£2,000 repair) and timing chain tensioner wear on pre-2010 examples (£1,500–£3,500 repair).

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Range Rover 4.4 V8 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: L322 values range from £3,000–£15,000 depending on condition and mileage. A £4,000–£6,500 reconditioned engine represents 25–65% of vehicle value — our 60% Land Rover scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained, post-2005 example, replacement is often justified. For a high-mileage pre-2005 car with other issues, assess scrap value first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Land Rover Range Rover 4.4 V8 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The L322 used two engines: BMW M62B44 (2002–2005, 282bhp) and Jaguar AJ-V8 4.4 (2005–2009, 305bhp). Check your VIN or registration year — pre-2005 is BMW, 2005–2009 is Jaguar. The code is stamped on the engine block.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the M62 timing chain guides, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. Timing chain guide replacement costs £2,000–£4,000 and is significantly cheaper than a full engine replacement (£3,000–£5,500). The job requires engine removal on the L322, making it a major but worthwhile investment. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately — ignored guide wear can lead to catastrophic piston-valve contact.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the later AJ-V8 era, the most common quote request today. BMW-era M62 pricing typically runs lower on the used market. See the Era Map above for era-specific context.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Range Rover 4.4 V8 engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £80–£120/hr (independent Land Rover specialist) or £140–£190/hr (London/SE dealer).

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Range Rover 4.4 V8 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Worth it on a well-maintained L322 with good body/transmission
Fit metal pipes for a permanent fix
Viable if caught early; rebuild often required
Replacement often £3,000–£6,500 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Range Rover 4.4 V8? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-2005 model with AJ-V8 — the better all-round engine in the L322
* AJ-V8 with documented timing chain and coolant pipe history
* M62 with timing chain guides already replaced
* Any 4.4 V8 with full service history and low-mileage (under 80,000)
avoidIf: * M62 (2002–2005) with no chain guide history — the risk is real
* AJ-V8 with evidence of coolant loss (crossover pipe failure imminent)
* Poor service history on either engine — both are unforgiving of neglect
* Any 4.4 with engine warning light and no diagnosis

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Range Rover 4.4 V8 is really two different engines sharing a badge. The BMW M62 (2002–2005) is a fine engine with a serious timing chain guide Achilles heel; the Jaguar AJ-V8 (2005–2009) is smoother, more reliable, and our recommended choice. Buy a 2005–2006 post-facelift car with the AJ-V8, check the coolant pipes, and maintain it properly — this is the sweet spot of L322 V8 ownership.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover 4.4 V8 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover 4.4 V8 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Any rattle? M62 chain guide rattle sounds metallic; AJ-V8 tensioner rattle is softer
* Service History — Check the year to identify which engine — pre-2005 = BMW, 2005+ = Jaguar
* Coolant Level — Check for any loss — AJ-V8 coolant pipes are the key risk
* Timing Chain History — M62: guides replaced? AJ-V8: tensioners checked?
* Oil Pressure — M62s can suffer from oil pump issues; listen for ticking
* VANOS Operation — On M62, check for low-end power; VANOS seals degrade
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Resolve before buying
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures (both engines can suffer catalyst/sensor issues)
